text: Debugging is a crucial skill for programmers. When your code doesn't work as expected, the first step is identifying the bug. You need to find the exact line or section causing the problem. Instead of guessing, use a systematic approach: check one part at a time. Helpful debugging tools include breakpoints in your editor, which pause the code so you can inspect variables. Another powerful tool is logging—adding print statements to track what your program is doing step by step. This allows you to see values and spot where things go wrong. With a clear strategy, you can solve bugs more efficiently and write better code.
